title: PSAP-Adjacent Overflow SOP
content: All Clear is CAD-adjacent triage and not a CAD replacement. Signals that appear to be non-emergency overflow may be triaged, but any immediate threat to life, active fire, crime in progress, or medical emergency must be escalated to the escalations queue and directed to public safety. Preserve the signal, avoid PII echo, and record whether a PSAP handoff was recommended. Sitreps must not claim dispatch status unless a source record provides unit status codes or CAD-adjacent confirmation.
snippet: Public-safety SOP for PSAP-adjacent overflow, escalation boundaries, and source discipline.

title: Public Safety Dispatch Handoff SOP
content: When RouterExecutor escalates a public-safety signal, the human handoff packet should include incident id, severity, queue, location, original signal timestamp, known hazards, and any unit status codes from source records. Do not add inferred details. If a unit is en route, on scene, 10-8, or 10-7, cite the status update source. ActionAgent cannot bypass escalation or modify dispatch records; it can only create the incident, search_knowledge, and generate a citation-grounded sitrep.
snippet: Dispatch handoff SOP for escalated public-safety incidents and bounded authority.

title: Evacuation Language Control SOP
content: Evacuation instructions must come from approved public-safety or field-operations source records. If a signal implies immediate danger but no approved evacuation language is available, escalate and say the system does not know rather than inventing instructions. Customer-comms may acknowledge receipt and advise contacting emergency services for immediate danger. The sitrep must cite this SOP and the hazard-specific SOP for any safety language included in a response.
snippet: Controls evacuation wording so safety advice is cited and escalation happens when knowledge is missing.
